sections.h:
1. Access the Automated Interview Plan (AIP) via the WA Police Intranet home page

via Forms, then Fill in a Form.

2. The Automated Interview Plan appears in the list of forms accessed from the

homepage.

3. The other access pathway is via the Investigative Standards intranet site, via the WA

Police Intranet site > The Job > Investigative Standards > Investigative Interviewing.

The link shown on this page will direct the user straight to the AIP.

4. Enter information via the first screen:

a. Incident Report Number (ddmmyy

hhmm nnnnn).

b. Interviewee’s Name and date of birth

(dd/mm/yyyy format).

c. Interviewer’s Name.

d. Corroborating Officer’s Name (if not

yet known, duplicate the Interviewer’s

details).

The interviewee’s date of birth will prefill the

‘Interviewee Information’ for Adult or

Child.

e. Make selections applicable to the circumstances of your interview.

The below example illustrates the selection of Adult with the Interviewee Status of Arrested Suspect.

The information detailed in the grey boxes detail the selections made in this example.
sections.b: 1. Access the Automated Interview Plan (AIP) via the WA Police Intranet home page via Forms, then Fill in a Form. 2. The Automated Interview Plan appears in the list of forms accessed from the homepage. 3. The other access pathway is via the Investigative Standards intranet site, via the WA Police Intranet site > The Job > Investigative Standards > Investigative Interviewing. The link shown on this page will direct the user straight to the AIP. 4. Enter information via the first screen: a. Incident Report Number (ddmmyy hhmm nnnnn). b. Interviewee’s Name and date of birth (dd/mm/yyyy format). c. Interviewer’s Name. d. Corroborating Officer’s Name (if not yet known, duplicate the Interviewer’s details). The interviewee’s date of birth will prefill the ‘Interviewee Information’ for Adult or Child. e. Make selections applicable to the circumstances of your interview. The below example illustrates the selection of Adult with the Interviewee Status of Arrested Suspect. The information detailed in the grey boxes detail the selections made in this example.

sections.h:
Yes selections to the following:

- Is there an interpreter

This selection will activate another selection area

of ‘Language nominated’ and in some

instances another selection field of ‘Was Pre-

recorded Caution used’.

All other considerations are indicated to No.

Pre-recorded Caution

Planning initiative for another

project, awaiting

implementation.
sections.b: Yes selections to the following: - Is there an interpreter This selection will activate another selection area of ‘Language nominated’ and in some instances another selection field of ‘Was Pre- recorded Caution used’. All other considerations are indicated to No. Pre-recorded Caution Planning initiative for another project, awaiting implementation.

sections.h:
‘Yes’ selections to the following:

Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander Person.

All other considerations are indicated to No.

5. The next screen is the ‘Interview Plan’

(only applicable for ‘Interview Status’ of

‘Arrested Suspect’ or ‘Accompanying

Person’).

The user has a choice to enter the

planning component of the ‘Interview

Plan’ electronically, or opt to manually

complete this after the AIP has

generated.

Here you can complete the planning

component of your suspect/accompanying

person interview, including Police Agenda

Steps, Evidence and Challenges.

6. The next screen will display a summary

of selections made.

For example, if your interviewee is an

‘Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander

Person’, and you did not select

‘Interview Friend’ as a consideration,

this will appear in red text as a reminder

to address the requirement.

It must be emphasised there are

considerations that must adhere to

legislative or case law requirements,

such as an interview of a juvenile.

In other instances, you can continue

without changing the consideration

selections, but you must document your

rationale on your running sheet, so the

decision can be explained at a later

date.

In this example, it may be that the consideration has been addressed by the interviewer, but is not required in this instance (e.g. Arrested Suspect did not wish for an Interview Friend to be present).

a) For corrections to any of your previous selections, click on the button marked ‘Previous’ and make the relevant choices, dependant on the circumstances of the interviewee.

Or

b) If all displayed selections are correct – Preview the information.

7. At this stage you can ‘Preview’ the information and forms generated into the

document, which is based on the selections made.

This is the last opportunity to make

changes to the information selected or to

amend incorrect text entries.

Use the ‘Exit’ button to move back

through the screens to make the required

amendments.

Note: the information will display with no

formatting to check the information

selections. This is the last opportunity to make any

amendments.

8. Select ‘Print’ and the document will generate as a PDF. The interview plan will appear

as a PDF document which you should ‘Save As’ to your office ‘P’ drive (or agency endorsed equivalent) under the appropriate folder for your investigation.

a. A message box will display at the bottom of the screen requesting whether you wish to ‘Open, Save or Cancel’.

b. Choose the ‘Save’ option.

i. Select the down arrow to the right of the

word ‘Save’

ii. Select ‘Save as’ to your office ‘P’

drive/equivalent under the appropriate

folder for your investigation

iii. Name the document

c. If you choose the ‘Save’ option, the PDF will

automatically save to your ‘Downloads’

folder; or

d. Choose the ‘Open’ option

i. The document will automatically name ‘AIP-[Interviewee Name]-[Date generated]’ and will be stored to the download folder on your computer, e.g.

‘AIP-Joe+Bloggs-19072021.pdf’.

If you have any questions or recommendations for this application please contact Investigative Standards Unit.

Note: the information will display with no formatting to check the information selections. This is the last opportunity to make any amendments. 8. The interview plan will appear as a PDF document which you should ‘Save As’ to your office ‘P’ drive (or agency endorsed equivalent) under the appropriate folder for your investigation. a. Select ‘Save as’ to your office ‘P’ drive/equivalent under the appropriate folder for your investigation iii. Name the document c. The document will automatically name ‘AIP-[Interviewee Name]-[Date generated]’ and will be stored to the download folder on your computer, e.g. ‘AIP-Joe+Bloggs-19072021.pdf’. If you have any questions or recommendations for this application please contact Investigative Standards Unit.

sections.h:
Arrested Suspect or Accompanying Person

Screen 1- Front Cover

Enter relevant information via the first screen

1. Interview Details

a. IR Number ddmmyy hhmm nnnnn

b. Interviewee’s Name Name of the interviewee

c. Date of birth dd/mm/yyyy format

(Determines interviewee as Adult or Child)

d. Interviewer’s Name Populates to the log-in details of the user d. Corroborating Officer’s Name Search using surname (pre-emptive text field), or if corroborator is unknown, duplicate

interviewer’s details.

2. Interview Status (only one choice is permitted)

a. Arrested Suspect;

b. Accompanying Person; or

c. Witness (not applicable for this interview status).

3. Interviewee Information (this is determined from the details entered for date of

birth)

a. Adult; or

b. Child.

Plus a ‘Y/N’ response to any combination of the following:

c. Aboriginal or Torres Strait Island Person.

d. Culturally or Linguistically Diverse.

e. Suspected to have Foetal Alcohol Spectrum Disorder (FASD).

f. Suspected mental impairment.

g. Physical impairment which may impact on communication.

h. Suspected involvement in recent emotional/traumatic incident.

i. Suspected recent drug/alcohol use.

4. Interview Considerations (only the relevant fields applicable to the Interviewee

Information are available)

a. Is there a responsible adult.

b. Is there an independent person.

c. Is there an interview friend.

d. Is there an interpreter (this selection will activate another selection area of ‘Language nominated’ and in some instances an additional selection field of ‘Was Pre-recorded Caution used’.

i. Language nominated (applicable to selection of ‘Interpreter’).

ii. Was Pre-recorded Caution used (applicable to selection of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander Person, and this field is required for future enhancements).

All the above listed fields require completion or the user will not be able to progress past Screen 1.

Screen 2 – Suspect Interview

(This screen only appears when Arrested Suspect/ Accompanying Person selected)

The user has the option of completing the ‘Planning’ requirements within the AIP:

a. Electronically; or

b. Manually.

Screen 3 - Summary

This screen will provide a summary of the selections made by the user.

Where there is a requirement to consider an option from Interview Considerations and this selection is not made, there will be information indicated in red text to warn the user that the associated relevant option has not been selected.

An example of this

a. Where ‘Culturally or Linguistically Diverse’ is selected on the initial screen; and b. ‘Is there an interpreter’ has not been selected on the initial screen.

This may have been an oversight with the initial selections, and the user has the option to go to the ‘Previous’ screen to amend the selections.

OR

The user may have determined that an interpreter is not required, and can progress to the next stage of the application. In this instance it is recommended the rationale and decision be recorded to the associated running sheet.

Screen 4 – Preview of AIP prior to generating

The AIP is viewable in a ‘Preview’ format.

a. The user has the capability to read or scan through the content of the entire document.

b. If the user notes changes to be made, there is a capability via the ‘Exit’ button to go to previous pages within the application to amend selections.

c. If the user determines the AIP is correct to proceed, progress to the final screen via the ‘Print’ button.

Important to note - Once the AIP is sent to print (generated PDF) the user will not be able to add or amend any information.

Screen 5 – Generated AIP document

A PDF document will be generated.

This document requires a ‘Save As’ to name and save to the designated electronic document repository (business unit drive).

The generated AIP will:

a. Produce a cover sheet listing:

i. IR Number

ii. Interviewee’s Name.

iii. Interviewer’s Name.

iv. Corroborating Officer’s Name.

v. Date of interview.

vi. Name of Person compiling.

vii. Date (indicates when the form was generated).

b. Produce and include information relevant to the selected ‘Interviewee Information’ (dependent on choices made) in the listed order:

i. Aboriginal or Torres Strait Island Person.

ii. Culturally or Linguistically Diverse.

iii. Suspected to have Foetal Alcohol Spectrum Disorder (FASD).

iv. Suspected mental impairment.

v. Physical impairment which may impact on communication.

vi. Suspected involvement in recent emotional/traumatic incident.

vii. Suspected recent drug/alcohol use.

c. Provide forms relevant to the ‘Interview Considerations’ and the sequence of interview. Information entered on the ‘Front Cover’ screen will populate into the relevant areas in the forms:

i. IR Number.

ii. Interviewee’s Name.

iii. Interviewer’s Name.

iv. Corroborating Officer’s Name.

Witness

Screen 1 – Front Cover

Enter relevant information via the first screen.

1. Interview Details

a. IR Number ddmmyy hhmm nnnnn

b. Interviewee’s Name. Name of the interviewee

c. Date of birth. dd/mm/yyyy format

(Determines Adult or Child)

d. Interviewer’s Name. Populates to the log-in details of the user

2. Interview Status (only one choice is permitted)

a. Arrested Suspect (not applicable for this interview status);

b. Accompanying Person (not applicable for this interview status); or c. Witness.

3. Interviewee Information (this is determined from the details entered for date of

birth)

a. Adult; or

b. Child.

Plus a ‘Y/N’ response to any combination of the following:

c. Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander Person.

d. Culturally or Linguistically Diverse.

e. Suspected to have Foetal Alcohol Spectrum Disorder (FASD).

f. Suspected mental impairment.

g. Physical impairment which may impact on communication.

h. Suspected involvement in recent emotional/traumatic incident.

i. Suspected recent drug/alcohol use.

4. Interview Considerations (only the relevant fields applicable to the selections

in ‘Interviewee Information’ are available)

a. Is there a responsible adult.

b. Is there an independent person.

c. Is there an interview friend.

d. Is there an interpreter (this selection will activate another selection area of ‘Language nominated’ and in some instances an additional selection field of ‘Was Pre-recorded Caution used’).

i. Language nominated (applicable to selection of interpreter).

ii. Was Pre-recorded Caution used (applicable to selection of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander Person).

The above listed fields require completion or the user will not be able to progress past Screen 1.

Screen 2 – Summary

This screen will provide a summary of the selections made by the user.

Where there is a requirement to consider an option from Interview Considerations and this selection is not made, there will be information indicated in red text to warn the user that the associated relevant option has not been selected.

An example of this

a. Where ‘Culturally or Linguistically Diverse’ is selected on the initial screen; and b. ‘Is there an interpreter’ has not been selected on the initial screen.

This may have been an oversight with the initial selections, and the user has the option to go to the ‘Previous’ screen to amend the selections.

OR

The user may have determined that an interpreter is not required, and can progress to the next stage. In this circumstance it is recommended the rationale and decision be recorded to the associated running sheet.

Screen 3 - Preview of AIP prior to generating

The AIP is viewable in a ‘Preview’ format.

a. The user has the capability to read or scan through the content of the entire document.

b. If the user notes changes to be made, there is a capability via the ‘Exit’ button to go to previous pages within the application to amend selections.

c. If the user determines the AIP is correct to proceed, progress to the final screen via the ‘Print’ button.

Important to note - Once the AIP is sent to print (generated PDF) the user will not be able to add or amend any information.

Screen 4 - Generated AIP document

A PDF document will be generated.

This document requires a ‘Save As’ to name and save to the designated electronic document repository (business unit drive).

The generated AIP will:

a. Produce a cover sheet listing:

i. IR Number

ii. Interviewee’s Name.

iii. Interviewer’s Name.

iv. Date of interview.

v. Name of Person compiling.

vi. Date (indicates when the form was generated).

b. Produce and include information relevant to the selected ‘Interviewee Information’ (dependent on choices made) in the listed order:

i. Aboriginal or Torres Strait Island Person.

ii. Culturally or Linguistically Diverse.

iii. Suspected to have Foetal Alcohol Spectrum Disorder (FASD).

iv. Suspected mental impairment.

v. Physical impairment which may impact on communication.

vi. Suspected involvement in recent emotional/traumatic incident.

vii. Suspected recent drug/alcohol use.

c. Provide forms relevant to the ‘Interview Considerations’ and the process of interview will collate to form the AIP. Information entered on the ‘Front Cover’ screen will populate into the relevant areas in the forms:

i. IR Number.

ii. Interviewee’s Name.

iii. Interviewer’s Name.

The document is formatted into two sections. The first section provides all of the information and resources to address known and suspected vulnerabilities of the interviewee. The second section is the suspect/accompanying person/witness guide for interview. You do not need to print the entire document for interview, only print the pages relevant to the interview guide.

sections.h:
Fact Sheet: Criminal Responsibility of Children

(doli incapax)
sections.b: Fact Sheet: Criminal Responsibility of Children (doli incapax)

sections.h:
This factsheet is intended for the internal use of the Western Australia Police Force only and is confidential and privileged. It must not be copied and/or provided externally without consulting Legal Services.

Scope The Prosecution must prove beyond

This factsheet addresses the age of criminal reasonable doubt that the accused had the responsibility under section 29 of the Criminal ‘capacity to know’ that they ‘ought not to do’ Code (WA) (Code), the presumption of doli the act they did.1 The Prosecution does not incapax (latin, meaning incapable of crime) have to prove that the child actually knew it and how this presumption can be rebutted. was seriously wrong to do the act/omission.2 This factsheet provides information and

examples of various ways for Investigating To rebut the presumption of incapacity the Officers (IOs) to rebut the presumption. Prosecution is required to prove that the child had the capacity to know what they were doing

Summary was wrong at the time of the offence, instead

It is presumed that a child between the ages of proving that they knew this generally.

of 10 and 14 cannot be criminally responsible.

However, this presumption can be rebutted if Seriously wrong or just naughty?

the Prosecution leads admissible evidence to The requirement for the Prosecution to persuade the court that the child had the establish ‘capacity to know’ exists to prevent capacity to know that their acts/omissions merely naughty children from being convicted were morally wrong by the standards of a of crimes and protect them from the reasonable adult. subsequent consequences of convictions.

Further, evidence led by the Prosecution must The Prosecution is to establish that the child be unequivocal and unambiguous. knows their act is not just ‘naughty’ for which they would be punished at home or school, but

The rebuttal of the presumption should be rather something that is ‘seriously wrong’ considered an element of the offence which requires punishment by a Court.3 during the investigative stage and form

part of the Evidence Matrix. Rebutting the presumption

IOs may use the following indicators to rebut

Section 29 of the Code the presumption with the following:
